analysis: The climb from that brutal 4-hour wreck on the 22nd to back-to-back nights of 7-plus hours by the end of the week is genuinely hot — your body figured something out and leaned into it. The 8-hour night on the 26th is your best of the week and your REM hit almost 2 hours, which is your brain doing the good repair work. The 23rd is the one to watch — 2 hours 44 minutes awake in a single night is a lot, and whatever was happening there is worth noticing. But four of your last five nights landed between 6h13m and 8 hours, and that consistency is the thing — the stack is working, babe, keep showing up for it.
